uplink.description: port 1, format 0x37, full payload: sd-card present+write-ok, vBat, vBus, boot, env (temp/RH), atmospheric pressure, two one-wire probe temps, two soil channels, two tree-pressure sensors, sap-flow + rain pulse counters, network timestamp
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds payload, format 0x37, flags1 = 0xFF (vBat 3.7 V, vBus 5.0 V, boot 25, t 22.5 degC, rh 60%, p 1013.24 hPa atmospheric, tProbeOne 23.8 degC, tProbeTwo 24.6 degC, soil#1 18.5 degC / 32.5% / type 0) and flags2 = 0x3F (soil#2 17.2 degC / 28% / type 1, tree-pressure#2 2.5 V, tree-pressure#1 2.8 V, sap pulse 1742 + rate, rain pulse 394 + rate, network timestamp). expected.data authored here; cross-checked against reference/upstream-codec.js (Decoder), which yields the same sdCardStatus/vBat/vBus/boot/t/rh/p/tProbeOne/tProbeTwo/soil#1/soil#2/sap/rain/timestamp values (plus the derived tDew/tHeatIndexC that we drop). tree-pressure values are derived here from the rounded rail volts (per sensor resolution), so they read 500 / 725 vs upstream's 500 / 725.0366.

uplink.description: port 4, format 0x37, minimal climate payload: sd-card present (write failed), battery, env (temp/RH), atmospheric pressure; flags2 empty
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds payload on port 4 (also a telemetry port), flags1 = 0x19 (vBat 3.65 V | env | pressure), flags2 = 0x00. sdCardStatus = 1 -> present, last write failed. expected.data authored here; cross-checked against reference/upstream-codec.js, which yields the same vBat (3.6499)/t (18.25)/rh (48.0003)/p (998.6) values (and the derived tDew we drop).
Port 3 carries downlink command responses (DecodeDownlinkResponse upstream), not sensor telemetry, so it is reported as an unsupported uplink port here.

uplink.description: supported port but unknown format byte -> error
uplink._note: Upstream Decoder returns null for any leading byte other than 0x37; we surface an explicit error.

uplink.description: supported port and format but truncated environment block -> error
uplink._note: flags1 = 0x08 (env present) needs 4 trailing bytes; only 1 is present. Upstream reads past the buffer (NaN); we report truncation.
